author: urn:li:organization:98086113
urn:li:organization:98086113
commentary:
The Invisible Risk in Modern Buildings

As buildings become more connected, the conversation around performance is evolving. It’s no longer limited to energy efficiency and aesthetics, our clients are asking how their physical environment supports digital resilience.

Electromagnetic transmission through glazing is now part of that discussion.
EMF window films offer a discreet way to reduce signal transmission through glass - often the most permeable element of the building envelope - while also providing glass containment benefits. For data centres, secure facilities, healthcare environments and high-spec commercial buildings, this is becoming a considered layer of protection rather than a specialist add-on.

We deliver high-performance EMF film solutions tailored to secure and mission-critical environments.

If you are reviewing building security, information protection or infrastructure resilience, it may be time to look at your glass differently.

Contact us for more information: info\@filmtekdatasolutions.com

author: urn:li:organization:98086113
commentary:
This week, our Technical Operations Manager, @[Gavin Bannerman](urn:li:person:y0E9qox69A), shared a perspective that clearly resonated within the data centre sector.

“It’s not a labour shortage. It’s a standards shortage.”

Across EMEA, data centre capacity is scaling at pace. Yet on many projects, the pressure is not simply about numbers on site, it is about whether the electrical workforce truly understands the environment in which they are operating.

There is a material difference between commercial installation and mission-critical delivery.

Between wiring a distribution board and wiring it to BS 7671 standard, achieving first-time inspection approval, and producing documentation that supports rather than delays commissioning.

Between pulling cable and understanding containment strategy, segregation requirements and the implications of design integrity within a Tier III facility.

Between completing a panel schedule and appreciating fault levels, discrimination studies and how each circuit integrates into a resilient power architecture where errors carry operational consequences.

Mission-critical projects do not tolerate ambiguity. They require technically competent, accountable electricians who understand not just how to install, but why the precision matters.

At FDS, our approach is built around deploying managed, qualified teams who are mission-critical literate and aligned to structured delivery environments.

As programme demands increase, so too must the standard of the workforce supporting them.

If your upcoming phases require electrical capability aligned to commissioning, compliance and clean handover, we welcome the discussion.

poll.question: Which electrical qualification do you currently hold?

commentary:
𝗝𝗜𝗕 / 𝗘𝗖𝗦 𝗘𝗹𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗦𝘂𝗽𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗖𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗲 𝗗𝗲𝗹𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘆

The demand for experienced electricians within data centre and mission-critical infrastructure projects continues to be more valuable. These environments require more than technical ability alone, demanding recognised qualifications and confidence operating within highly controlled, compliance-driven settings.

We are currently engaging with JIB and ECS-qualified electricians who have experience in critical infrastructure, white space installations, plant rooms and commissioning support.

If you are operating within the mission-critical sector or looking to align your experience with upcoming data centre programmes we would welcome a conversation.

You may also register your interest here: https://wkf.ms/49UFACP
